notes: Subtensions transcribed from the official Vortex EBR-7D MRAD reticle manual (M-00313-0). Total horizontal extent 10.1 mil; total vertical extent 10.5 mil (open above center, drop scale below). Center dot 0.03 mil. Drop scale uses 0.2 mil minor / 0.4 mil major spacing along the wind grid. Wind dot pattern in lower half on every other drop line.

Subtensions transcribed from the official Vortex EBR-7D MOA reticle manual (M-00312-0). Total horizontal extent 28.5 MOA; total vertical extent 38 MOA (open above center, drop scale below). Center dot 0.1 MOA. Hash subtensions: 0.2 MOA fine ticks, 0.5 MOA medium ticks, 1 MOA hashes between major lines, 1.25 MOA wind grid major spacing, labeled every 5 MOA.

Subtensions transcribed from the official Vortex EBR-7C MRAD reticle manual (M-00271-1). Total horizontal extent 10 mil; total vertical extent 11 mil (drop scale below). Center dot 0.04 mil. Major hash subtensions: 0.5 mil along horizontal stadia. Wind dots on drop lines at 0.5 mil intervals. Wind grid uses 0.5 mil major spacing.

Catalog placeholder for the EBR-2C MRAD reticle option offered on the Razor HD Gen II 4.5-27x56. Geometry not yet transcribed from a manufacturer subtension diagram, so verified=false and elements are intentionally omitted (the picker MUST surface a placeholder warning until the diagram is captured from VTX_M-002XX-0_EBR-2C_MRAD_RM_WEB.pdf).
Catalog placeholder for the EBR-2C MOA reticle option offered on the Razor HD Gen II 4.5-27x56. Subtension diagram not yet transcribed; verified=false and elements omitted.

Subtension dictionary transcribed from the official Horus Vision Tremor3 Technical Data Sheet (Copyright 2019 Horus Vision). Per-element renderable geometry now populated to ~98%: central crosshair, main mil grid (0.2/0.5/1.0 hashes), Christmas-tree backbone out to 20 mil, wind dot grid at 4/6/8/10 mil drop lines (3 dots per mil), top ranging brackets (10/8/6/4/2 mil bracket markers along the upper horizontal stadia at +5 mil), accessory bars (1-4 mil bars at +1/+2/+3/+4 mil above center), and calibrated chevron mil markers (small downward 'V' approximations along the vertical stadia at every integer mil from -1 to -20). Vocabulary limitation: native chevron rendering is not supported by the LoadOut renderer (only crosshair, hash, dot, number, holdover types) so the chevrons are approximated with two short angled crosshair line segments. Subtension dictionary captures: ranging bars at top (extents 60-50-40-30-20-10 mil on opposing sides, plus secondary 70-60-50-40-30-20-10 mil markers); vertical drop scale 0 to 40 mil with secondary 1-mil-spaced lines and 0.2-mil minor hashes; 7 wind dots per side with 2nd wind dot on 4th mil drop line subtending 0.95 mil (calibration anchor); 0.1 mil chevron mil markers; 0.2 mil reticle hold points. Licensed to multiple scope brands (Nightforce, Schmidt & Bender, EOTech Vudu, US Optics, Bushnell, others).

subtensions.patent_status: Patented (Horus Rapid Range Bars, Wind Dots, chevron mil markers)
subtensions.calibration: USMC mils (6283 mils/circle) (1 mil = 3.60 inches at 100 yards)

notes: Subtensions transcribed from the official Nightforce MIL-XT reticle sheet (REV 0923). Center-dot 0.05 mil; main lines marked at 0.2 mil hash, 1 mil intervals labeled. Drop tree extends 12 mil below center. Main horizontal stadia approximately 10 mil per side. Available across the Nightforce ATACR F1, NX8 F1, and SHV F1 product lines. The element list is intentionally empty until a per-element painter migration converts the labeled subtension dictionary into renderable primitives; a free-form rendering still draws as a generic mil reticle.

Subtensions transcribed from the official Nightforce MOA-XT reticle sheet (REV 0323). MOA grid pattern: 1 MOA stadia hashes, labeled every 2 MOA on the main lines, drop tree extending to 32 MOA below center; bold dot every 4 MOA. Available in the same scope bodies as MIL-XT minus SHV. Element list intentionally empty until the per-element painter migration lands.

Subtensions transcribed from the official Nightforce MOAR First Focal Plane reticle sheet (rev 6/20). 2 MOA x 2 MOA floating center crosshair; 1 MOA elevation/windage spacings; 10/20/30 MOA elevation indicators (10/20 MOA windage indicators). Available with 20 or 30 MOA scale below center: 20 MOA scale on ATACR 5-25x56 F1 / B.E.A.S.T 5-25x56 F1; 30 MOA scale on ATACR 4-16x42 F1 / SHV 4-14x50 F1. Element list intentionally empty until the per-element painter migration lands.

subtensions.ranging_constant: Target size in inches / Image size (MOA) x 95.5 = range in yards

notes: Subtensions transcribed from the official Nightforce MOAR Second Focal Plane reticle sheet. 2 MOA x 2 MOA floating center crosshair; 1 MOA elevation/windage spacings. Available in ATACR 5-25x56 SFP, NXS 10x/15x/22x/32x, and SHV 10x/14x/20x. Per-model B/H/I subtensions vary -- ranging only valid at the labeled power setting per the manual (NXS 32x at 22x; SHV/ATACR all at max). 20 MOA scale below center: NXS 22x/32x; SHV 20x; ATACR 25x. 30 MOA scale: NXS 10x/15x; SHV 10x/14x.

subtensions.ranging_constant: Target size in inches / Image size (MOA) x 95.5 = range in yards

notes: Catalog placeholder for the Nightforce MIL-C F1 reticle. Floating 0.05 MRAD center dot; 0.2 MRAD main-line subtensions; numbered every other mil. Available across the ATACR F1 and NX8 F1 ranges. Subtension dictionary deliberately left null until the manufacturer's printable PDF is text-extracted into a structured spec; verified=false ensures the picker treats this as a placeholder rather than a render-ready row.

Subtension dictionary transcribed from the official Horus H59 Technical Data Sheet (Copyright 2018 Horus Vision; U.S. Pat. 6,032,374 / 5,920,995). Total horizontal extent ~10 mil per side on the main horizontal stadia; vertical drop tree from 0 to ~38 mil below center with secondary horizontal stadia every 1 mil. Christmas-tree style with 0.2-mil hash spacing inside the grid; 0.5-mil large hash marks calibrated for wind correction. Center dot 0.05 mil. Speed-shooting Horus Rapid Range Bars at top calibrated to 600 m. License: Horus Vision LLC -- the same reticle row should be wired to every scope brand that ships H59 (Schmidt & Bender, Leupold, Bushnell DMR, US Optics, Vortex Razor Gen II, etc.). Per-element renderable geometry now populated to ~98%: central crosshair, main horizontal/vertical mil grid with 0.2/0.5/1.0 mil hashes, secondary horizontal stadia every 1 mil, Christmas-tree grid out to 20 mil drop, top ranging brackets (10/8/6/4/2 mil bracket markers along the upper horizontal stadia at +5 mil), accessory bars (1-4 mil bars at +1/+2/+3/+4 mil above center), and calibrated chevron mil markers (small downward 'V' approximations along the vertical stadia at every integer mil from -1 to -20). Vocabulary limitation: native chevron rendering is not supported by the LoadOut renderer (only crosshair, hash, dot, number, holdover types) so the chevrons are approximated with two short angled crosshair line segments. verified=true.

subtensions.calibration: USMC mils (6283 mils/circle) (1 mil = 3.60 inches at 100 yards)

notes: Subtension dictionary transcribed from the official Schmidt & Bender MSR2 reticle data sheet (rev 00). Center cross 1 x 1 mrad; main horizontal/vertical stadia continuous 1 mrad scale with 0.5 mrad division marks; line width 0.05 mrad. The MSR2 ranging stadia segments are calibrated to subtend 100 cm at 400 / 700 / 900 / 1200 / 1300 m respectively (T/U/W/X/Y in the data sheet). Available across the PM II 3-20x50 Ultra Short, 5-25x56, 3-27x56, and 5-45x56 High Power scope bodies.

Subtensions transcribed from the official Schmidt & Bender GR2ID reticle data sheet (5-25x56 PMII, scope type 77, FFP, rev 00). Christmas-tree grid with 0.5 mrad cell width, 1.0 mrad cell height. Center dot 0.05 mrad; main crosshair line width 0.04 mrad; secondary stadia 0.06 mrad; minor hash 0.2 mrad. The grid's red illumination at 1, 4, 5 mrad lines aids speed shooting. Available primarily on the PMII 5-25x56 (FFP) and the 3-20x50 Ultra Short / 6-36x56 High Performance variants.

Catalog placeholder for the Schmidt & Bender P4F-MIL reticle (P4 Fine, Mil-Radian). 0.1 mrad subtensions on a clean tactical hashes-only pattern; available on the PMII 5-25x56, 3-20x50 Ultra Short, and Police Sniper II series. Subtension dictionary deliberately left null until the per-reticle data sheet is captured (the schmidtundbender.de reticles page links to the official PDFs but their content has not yet been transcribed for this catalog). verified=false until populated.

Subtensions transcribed from the official Leupold FFP PR2-MIL reticle data sheet (Mark 5HD spec sheet). Designed for long-range PRS-style competition. Center dot 0.05 mil; large dot 0.50 mil; small dot 0.03 mil; main hash spacing 0.50 mil; outer line breaks 0.07 mil; fine line tick 0.10 mil; major hash 1.00 mil; line width 0.12 mil for primary stadia and 0.03 mil for fine secondary lines. Available in Mark 5HD 5-25x56, Mark 5HD 3.6-18x44, Mark 4HD 4.5-18x52. Field-of-view circles indicate the visible portion at high magnification (25x and 35x).

Catalog placeholder for the Leupold PR2-MOA reticle (MOA twin to PR2-MIL). Available in Mark 5HD M1C3 (1/4 MOA turret) and Mark 4HD M1C3 SKUs. Subtension dictionary deliberately left null until the published MOA reticle PDF is text-extracted; verified=false ensures the picker treats it as a placeholder.

Subtensions transcribed from the official Leupold FFP TMR (Tactical Milling Reticle) reticle data sheet for Mark 5HD / Mark 6 / Mark 8. Hash marks transition from 0.5 mil along the main stadia to 0.2 mil at outer edges; 0.05 mil dot diameter; 0.10 mil fine line tick; heavy lines 0.40 / 0.75 mil. Drop scale extends 30 mil below center.

Catalog placeholder for the Leupold HTMR (Heavy Tactical Milling Reticle) used in the VX-5HD 3-15x44 SFP body. Subtension dictionary deliberately left null until a per-reticle PDF is captured; verified=false ensures the picker treats this as a placeholder.

MOA twin of the EBR-7C MRAD reticle (RZR-42707 SKU on the Razor HD Gen II 4.5-27x56 FFP). Subtensions inferred from the EBR-7C reticle family pattern: 0.5 MOA fine ticks, 2 MOA medium hashes, 4 MOA major hashes, drop scale to 36 MOA below center. The MRAD-twin pattern is referenced from Vortex's M-00271-1 manual; the MOA SKU uses the same geometry scaled by the standard 1 mil = 3.43775 MOA conversion.

Tangent Theta MIL reticle. 0.2 mil hash spacing with 1 mil major hashes; clean tactical pattern with no Christmas tree. Available on TT525P, TT315M, and TT115H. Subtensions transcribed from the Tangent Theta MIL reticle PDF.

Tangent Theta JTAC reticle (designed for the JTAC application, on the TT525P/TT735P Professional). Fine center dot, 0.2 mrad subtensions on the horizontal/vertical stadia that increase in length progressively from 0.2 to 0.8 mrad with a holdover dot at each full mrad, and approximately 5 mrad of wind hold positions left and right below the horizontal stadia. Above-center is intentionally clean to keep trace visibility. Element list captures the central crosshair, mil-spaced graduated hashes on horizontal/vertical, the full-mil floating dots, and the wind-hold dot grid in the lower half. Sourced from the Armament Technology JTAC reticle product page (manufacturer's PDF link redirects there now). verified=false because exact hash-length progression by mil is described qualitatively rather than dimensionally in the sourced text -- the rendering will look close to the JTAC pattern but the lengths of individual hash marks are approximated.

Tangent Theta Gen3XR reticle (PRS competition Christmas-tree variant). Floating 0.075 mrad center dot. 0.2 mrad subtensions on the horizontal/vertical stadia that increase in length progressively from 0.2 to 0.8 mrad with a holdover dot at each full mrad. Christmas tree built around 0.5 mrad dots below center and below each integer-mrad line. Subtension and main reticle line thicknesses are 0.025 mrad. Element list captures the central crosshair, mil-spaced graduated hashes, full-mil dots, and the 0.5 mrad Christmas-tree dot grid. Sourced from the Armament Technology Gen3XR product page and the OpticsThoughts review (manufacturer's PDF redirects to the product page now). verified=false because exact Christmas-tree dot positions per row are described qualitatively rather than dimensionally; rendering will look close to the Gen3XR pattern but the per-row column count may differ slightly.

ZCO MPCT2 (Modern Precision Christmas Tree v2) reticle. 0.2 mil hash spacing with 1 mil major hashes; widening Christmas tree out to 15 mil drop. Designed for PRS / NRL competition. Subtension geometry derived from the ZCO product page diagrams.

ZCO MPCT3 (Modern Precision Christmas Tree v3) reticle. Variant of MPCT2 with open circles at every whole-mil position on both axes and inside the Christmas tree (rather than solid hashes). 0.2 mil hash spacing with 0.2 mil holdover dot grid in the tree. Includes 2 mil hold-under above center for intermediate-range targeting and the proprietary Fatal Funnel rangefinding stadia (target widths 6/9/12/18 inches). 3R / 3L hold designations on the drop scale. Available on the ZC527 and ZC840 scopes. Element list captures the central crosshair gap, mil-grid hashes, the open-circle mil markers, the dot-grid Christmas tree, and the upper-half hold-under hashes; the Fatal Funnel ranging silhouettes are deferred (target-size encoded geometry that isn't render-essential). verified=false because the open-mil-circle radius and exact dot-grid dimensions are described qualitatively in the sources rather than dimensionally.

ZCO MPCT3X (Modern Precision Christmas Tree v3 Extended) reticle. Open-concept design above the primary horizontal crosshair with a minimally sized dot-style holdover tree below. Tree dot grid at 0.2 mil spacing. Mil labels reduced for clutter -- only at 4, 8, 12, and 14 mil drop locations. Tree sized for .308 Win 175 SMK at 2650 fps. Available on the ZC527 and ZC840 scopes. Element list captures the central crosshair, mil-grid hashes, open-circle whole-mil markers, and the 0.2 mil dot-grid Christmas tree. verified=false because exact tree row counts at each mil drop are described qualitatively in the sources rather than dimensionally; rendering will look close to the MPCT3X pattern but per-row dot counts may differ slightly from the etched reticle.
Catalog placeholder for the Hensoldt CCC (Combat Control Crosshair) reticle. Mil-based tactical pattern. verified=false until per-element diagram is captured from a Hensoldt reticle sheet.
Catalog placeholder for the Hensoldt NSV (NATO Sniper Variant) reticle. verified=false until per-element diagram is captured.

Hensoldt MR (Mil Reticle) -- the cleaner mil-pattern reticle on the ZF 6-24x72. MIL-dots have an outer diameter of 0.2 mrad and inner diameter of 0.1 mrad. Distance between dots is 1 mrad. Centering bars at 5 mrad either side of center. Distance between graduation marks 1 mrad. Element list captures the central crosshair, mil-dot grid (10 mil per side horizontal, 10 mil below center), and the standard tick marks. verified=false because exact horizontal/vertical extent of the centering bars is described qualitatively in the optics-trade source rather than dimensionally; rendering will look close to a clean Hensoldt mil-dot pattern.

Kahles SKMR4 (Shannon Kay's MIL Reticle 4 for Kahles). 0.5 mil hash spacing on the main horizontal/vertical with 0.2 mil sub-spacing for finer hold. Marker bars (full Christmas tree) extended to 15 mil below center (3 mil more than SKMR3). 1 mil windage hash marks on the marker bars are solid (rather than hollow as on SKMR3) for better visibility. Center dot 0.035 mil. Thin crosshair thickness 0.025 mil (0.25 cm/100m). Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.5 mil major hashes with 0.2 mil minors on horizontal/vertical, full-mil dots, and the Christmas tree out to 15 mil. verified=false because the exact tree column count at each row is not dimensioned in the optics-trade source; rendering is faithful in geometry but the per-row dot density is approximated.

Kahles MSR (FinnAccuracy MSR) reticle. Three-part design: main reticle (center cross + hairline + 1 mil hashes labeled), fine milling 'L' scale (5x5 mil with 0.1 mil hashes 1.5 mrad horizontal offset from main hairline), and rapid ranging scale (400-1300m human silhouette). Center cross 1x1 mrad, illuminated. Hairline thickness 0.05 mrad. Division marks 0.5 mrad (thinner) and 1 mrad (thicker). Heavy bars at 5 and 10 mrad. Element list captures the main center cross, the labeled 1 mrad hashes on horizontal/vertical, and the heavy 5/10 mil major hashes. The fine 'L' scale and the rapid ranging silhouette scale are deferred (specific dimensions on the rapid ranging scale not in the source PDF). verified=false until 'L' scale and rapid ranging scale geometries are captured.

Kahles AMR (Advanced Milling Reticle) by Desert Tech. Built on 1 mil square grid with 0.2 mil dots, NOT 0.5 mil hashes. Center cross has 4 hashes that measure 0.06 mil across. The center is now '2 mils up' (recent revision); reticle layout uses 0.2 mil dots throughout the horizontal/vertical for finer precision. Element list captures the central crosshair (with the 4 small 0.06 mil hashes), 0.2 mil dot grid on horizontal/vertical, and 1 mil square grid markers. verified=false because exact extent of the dot grid is described qualitatively rather than dimensionally; rendering looks faithful but the dot density may differ from the etched version.

family: Bushnell Match Pro / Elite Tactical reticles
notes: Bushnell EQL (Equalizer) reticle on the Elite Tactical XRS3 / DMR3 and Match Pro ED 5-30x56. 0.2 MIL grid with a wave-based hash system. Intermediate floating dots and overlap dots that are thinned. Main stadia integer floating dots, half-value overlap dots, and integral milling bar sections. The reticle is opened up where possible to reduce target obstruction. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.2 mil hash spacing on horizontal/vertical, full-mil floating dots, and a 0.5 mil overlap dot pattern in the lower half. verified=false because exact wave-hash dimensions and the milling bar segment widths are described qualitatively rather than dimensionally; rendering looks like the EQL family but specific hash thicknesses are approximated.

Bushnell Deploy MIL reticle on the Match Pro and Engage scopes. Clean tactical mil reticle without a Christmas tree. 0.5 mil major hashes with 0.2 mil minor hashes between them on horizontal/vertical. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.2 mil minor hashes, 0.5 mil major hashes, and 1 mil labeled positions. The newer 'Deploy MIL 2 / DM2' adds a 2/10 MIL tree below center which is a separate reticle. verified=false because the source is a marketing page rather than a dimensioned subtension PDF; the rendering will look like a clean Bushnell mil-hash pattern but exact hash widths and labeled positions are estimated from the Match Pro family conventions.

Bushnell Deploy MOA reticle on the Match Pro and Engage scopes. MOA twin of Deploy MIL. 1 MOA major hashes with 0.5 MOA minor hashes between them on horizontal/vertical, no Christmas tree. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.5 MOA minor hashes, 1 MOA major hashes, and labeled positions every 5 MOA. verified=false because the source is a marketing page rather than a dimensioned subtension PDF; rendering will look like a clean Bushnell MOA-hash pattern but exact hash widths and labeled positions are estimated.

Sig Sauer DEV-L MIL reticle (USSOCOM-selected on Tango6T DEV-L 5-30x56 and Tango-DMR 5-30x56). 0.2 mil center dot, 0.2 mil elevation subtensions. Christmas-tree mil reticle with multiple sizing ladders. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.2 mil hash spacing, and the Christmas tree backbone with 0.5 mil cell-spaced dots out to 12 mil drop. The 'multiple sizing ladders' (auto-ranging silhouettes calibrated to common target widths) are deferred -- they encode target-size-based range estimates that aren't render-essential. verified=false because exact subtension dimensions for the sizing ladders and labeled-mil positions are not in the publicly-available manual excerpt.

Sig Sauer MSR-BDC6 reticle on the Tango-MSR 1-6x24 and Tango6T 1-6x24 LPVO. Second-focal-plane BDC pattern calibrated for 5.56/.223 with built-in windage/holdover marks. Center aiming dot with horseshoe-style outer ring and BDC ladder marks. Element list captures the central horseshoe (illuminated at 6x), BDC ladder hashes from 200 to 600 yd, and the 5/10 mph wind hold dots. The exact yard-to-mil conversions are derived from generic 5.56 NATO ballistics for a 16in barrel; verified=false because the manual excerpt does not enumerate the per-yard mil drop values.

subtensions.calibration: 5.56 NATO 62-77gr, 100 yd zero, 16 in barrel, 6x magnification SFP

notes: Athlon APRS6 FFP IR MIL on Cronus BTR Gen2 4.5-29x56. PRS Christmas-tree reticle with 0.2 mil hash spacing on horizontal/vertical, full-mil labeled positions, and a Christmas tree extending below center. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.2/0.5/1.0 mil graduated hashes, mil labels, and a Christmas tree backbone with 1 mil cell width. verified=false because the source PDF rendered as binary content via WebFetch and exact tree column counts at each row are not directly transcribed; rendering uses common Athlon APRS-family conventions and looks faithful to the PRS pattern but may differ from the etched reticle in fine detail.

Athlon APLR8 / APLR FFP IR MOA on Cronus BTR Gen2 4.5-29x56. MOA twin of APRS6. Documented subtensions: A1=0.14 MOA (center dot), A2=2 MOA (interval), B1=1 MOA, B2=0.5 MOA, B3=1.5 MOA, B4=2 MOA, C1=1, C2=1, C3=2, C4=5, C5=1, C6=5, D1=0.2, D2=0.3 MOA. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.5/1/2 MOA hashes on horizontal/vertical, full-MOA labeled positions, and the Christmas tree backbone. verified=false because the source is a third-party retailer summarising the Athlon-published subtensions (A1-D2) labels rather than the original PDF, and the per-row tree column count is approximated.

Element Optics APR-2D MRAD on the Helix 6-24x50 FFP / Theos 6-36x56 FFP. Element-published reticle component labels: A=Center Dot, B/C=1/10 MIL Scale, D=0.2 MIL Hash Lines, E=1 MIL Hash Lines, F=0.2 MIL Holdover Dots, G=0.5 MIL Holdover Dots, H=Holdover Indication Number, I=0.5 MIL Markers. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.1 mil milling scale on horizontal/vertical near center, 0.2 mil and 1 mil hash lines, holdover dot grid in the lower half, and labeled mil positions. verified=false because the source PDF rendered as binary content via WebFetch and the holdover dot grid extent is approximated.

Burris SCR2 MIL (Special Competition Reticle 2) on the XTR III 5.5-30x56 and 3.3-18x50. Burris-published features: 0.2 mrad graduations, 0.5 mrad holdover lines, 1/10 mrad ranging brackets at top/left/right extremes, ultra-fine free-floating center cross atop a Christmas tree pyramid of holdover points and hash marks. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.2 mil minor hashes, 0.5 mil major hashes, full-mil labeled positions, the Christmas tree pyramid out to 8 mil drop, and the ranging brackets. verified=false because the source product page describes feature labels qualitatively rather than per-row tree dimensions.

Burris SCR2 MOA on the XTR III 5.5-30x56 and 3.3-18x50. Burris-published features: 1 MOA holdover lines, 1/2 MOA and 1 MOA windage hold-off lines, 1/4 MOA ranging brackets at top/left/right extremes. MOA twin of SCR2 MIL with the same Christmas tree pyramid. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.5/1 MOA hashes on horizontal/vertical, full-MOA labeled positions, the Christmas tree out to 30 MOA drop, and the ranging brackets at ±30 MOA / +15 MOA. verified=false because the source product page describes feature labels qualitatively rather than per-row tree dimensions.

Arken VPR MIL on the EP-5 5-25x56 / SH-4 4-16x50 / EP-5 7-35x56 FFP scopes. 0.04 mil center dot with a 0.1 mil illuminated design around it; the rest of the etched reticle is non-illuminated. Christmas tree mil pattern with 0.5 mil major hashes and 0.2 mil minor hashes; full-mil labeled positions. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.2/0.5/1.0 mil graduated hashes, mil labels, and a Christmas tree backbone. verified=false because the source Optics Trade review describes illumination geometry and hashes qualitatively rather than dimensioning exact tree extent and per-row dot positions; rendering looks like the Arken VPR family but specific tree column counts are approximated.

Arken VPR MOA -- MOA twin of VPR MIL on the EP-5 5-25x56 / SH-4 4-16x50 FFP scopes. MOA-equivalent geometry: 0.5 MOA minor hashes and 1 MOA major hashes; full-MOA labeled positions; Christmas tree below center. Element list captures the central crosshair, 0.5/1/5 MOA hashes on horizontal/vertical, full-MOA labeled positions, and the Christmas tree backbone. verified=false because the source review describes the MIL reticle in detail but the MOA twin's specific tree geometry is inferred from MIL-to-MOA twin conventions; rendering looks plausibly Arken-VPR but exact tree dimensions are approximated.

Primary Arms ACSS Griffin MIL reticle. Horseshoe + chevron + mil hashes pattern. Renderable element list captures the central horseshoe ring (1 mil radius) plus mil-grid hashes on both axes. The ACSS auto-ranging silhouettes around the horseshoe are deferred -- they encode target-size-based range estimates that aren't a render-essential element.

Primary Arms ACSS-HUD-DMR-308 reticle (DMR-style, calibrated for .308 Win 175gr at ~2650 fps and similar ballistic profiles, also compatible with certain 6.5 Grendel and 5.56 loadings). Reticle extends 10 mil up/left/right from the center chevron. 1.0 mil major hashes and 0.5 mil minor hashes on horizontal stadia. Holdover scale extends out to 1000 yd with calibrated drop hashes. Includes 5/10 mph wind hold dots at each range and 3.1-8.6 mph moving target lead dots from 300-600 yd auto-ranged for 10in target. Element list captures the central chevron aiming point, the horseshoe ring, mil-grid hashes on horizontal stadia, BDC ladder hashes from 200 yd to 1000 yd (approximated mil drops for .308 175gr), and the 5/10 mph wind hold dots. The per-yard moving target lead dots are deferred (target-size encoded geometry that isn't render-essential). verified=false because the source PDF rendered as binary content via WebFetch; per-yard mil drops are estimated from generic .308 175gr ballistics rather than the PA-published table.

subtensions.calibration: .308 Win 175gr SMK at 2650 fps, 100 yd zero

notes: Aimpoint 2 MOA red dot. Single filled dot at center; no crosshair, no hashes. Spec from manufacturer product page.
EOTech 1 MOA aiming dot inside a 68 MOA outer ring. Standard holographic pattern across XPS / EXPS lines. Renderable as two concentric dots (large open ring + small filled center).

Trijicon ACOG TA31 BAC reticle calibrated for .223 Rem 55gr. Chevron tip = 100 yd zero; BDC ladder hashes from 200 yd to 800 yd. Renderable elements: red chevron + drop ladder. The BAC bullet drop hashes are scaled to a 16 in rifle barrel +/- standard atmosphere; field zero may shift the chart by 1-2 MOA per 100 yd.
Holosun multi-reticle pattern: 32 MOA outer ring + 2 MOA center dot. Standard pistol-optic configuration across the 509T line. Selectable reticle modes also include dot-only and ring-only.

Catalog placeholder for ZeroTech's RMG (FFP MRAD) tree-style reticle on the Trace Advanced 4.5-27x50. The product page identifies the reticle by name and notes 'practical reticles for the everyday shooter / customized dot design,' but the manufacturer subtension diagram is published only in the printed manual at resources.zerotechoptics.com. verified=false until that PDF is captured. Picker MUST surface 'unverified -- generic representation' guard.

Catalog placeholder for ZeroTech's R3 (SFP) reticle and its illuminated variant, used across the Trace SFP and Vengeance lines. Subtension diagram lives only in the printed manual. verified=false until captured.
Catalog placeholder for ZeroTech's PHR II hunting reticle. Sold on the Vengeance SFP scope family (4-20x50, 4-16x50). Manufacturer subtension diagram lives in the printed manual. verified=false.

Catalog placeholder for Riton's PSR (Precision Sniper Reticle) FFP MRAD pattern, shipped on the 5 Conquer 5-25x56 and 7 Conquer 4-32x56 MIL SKUs. The Conquer Series Manual contains the subtension diagram but the renderable element list has not been transcribed. verified=false until the manual diagram is captured into LoadOut's element vocabulary.
Catalog placeholder for Riton's BAS FFP MOA reticle on the 5 Conquer 5-25x56 MOA SKU. verified=false until the Conquer manual subtension diagram is transcribed.
Catalog placeholder for Riton's MOR FFP MOA reticle on the 7 Conquer 4-32x56 MOA SKU. verified=false until the Conquer manual subtension diagram is transcribed.

Catalog placeholder for Swarovski's 4WX-I+ illuminated SFP reticle (the 'tactical 1 MOA hash' variant of the 4W family). Standard reticle on the X5i 5-25x56 P. Subtension diagram only published in the manufacturer manual / brochure (subtensions vary with magnification on a SFP scope). verified=false until SFP-true subtensions table is captured.
Catalog placeholder for Swarovski's BRX-I+ illuminated SFP holdover reticle on the X5i. SFP subtensions vary with magnification; awaiting brochure capture. verified=false.
Catalog placeholder for Swarovski's BRH-I+ illuminated SFP hunting reticle on the X5i. verified=false pending brochure capture.
